NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. To bring to remembrance. O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your wrath, Nor chasten me in Your hot displeasure!
NKJV.2: For Your arrows pierce me deeply, And Your hand presses me down.
NKJV.3: There is no soundness in my flesh Because of Your anger, Nor any health in my bones Because of my sin.
NKJV.4: For my iniquities have gone over my head; Like a heavy burden they are too heavy for me.
NKJV.5: My wounds are foul and festering Because of my foolishness.
NKJV.6: I am troubled, I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
NKJV.7: For my loins are full of inflammation, And there is no soundness in my flesh.
NKJV.8: I am feeble and severely broken; I groan because of the turmoil of my heart.
NKJV.9: Lord, all my desire is before You; And my sighing is not hidden from You.
NKJV.10: My heart pants, my strength fails me; As for the light of my eyes, it also has gone from me.
NKJV.11: My loved ones and my friends stand aloof from my plague, And my relatives stand afar off.
NKJV.12: Those also who seek my life lay snares for me; Those who seek my hurt speak of destruction, And plan deception all the day long.
NKJV.13: But I, like a deaf man, do not hear; And I am like a mute who does not open his mouth.
NKJV.14: Thus I am like a man who does not hear, And in whose mouth is no response.
NKJV.15: For in You, O LORD, I hope; You will hear, O Lord my God.
NKJV.16: For I said, “Hear me, lest they rejoice over me, Lest, when my foot slips, they exalt themselves against me.”
NKJV.17: For I am ready to fall, And my sorrow is continually before me.
NKJV.18: For I will declare my iniquity; I will be in anguish over my sin.
NKJV.19: But my enemies are vigorous, and they are strong; And those who hate me wrongfully have multiplied.
NKJV.20: Those also who render evil for good, They are my adversaries, because I follow what is good.
NKJV.21: Do not forsake me, O LORD; O my God, be not far from me!
NKJV.22: Make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation!

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>, to bring to remembrance<S>2142</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S>, rebuke<S>3198</S> me not in thy wrath<S>7110</S>: neither chasten<S>3256</S> me in thy hot displeasure<S>2534</S>.
KJV.2: For thine arrows<S>2671</S> stick fast<S>5181</S> in me, and thy hand<S>3027</S> presseth me sore<S>5181</S>.
KJV.3: There is no soundness<S>4974</S> in my flesh<S>1320</S> because<S>6440</S> of thine anger<S>2195</S>; neither is there any rest<S>7965</S> in my bones<S>6106</S> because<S>6440</S> of my sin<S>2403</S>. <sup>rest: Heb. peace, or, health</sup>
KJV.4: For mine iniquities<S>5771</S> are gone over<S>5674</S> mine head<S>7218</S>: as an heavy<S>3515</S> burden<S>4853</S> they are too heavy<S>3513</S> for me.
KJV.5: My wounds<S>2250</S> stink<S>887</S> and are corrupt<S>4743</S> because<S>6440</S> of my foolishness<S>200</S>.
KJV.6: I am troubled<S>5753</S>; I am bowed down<S>7817</S> greatly<S>3966</S>; I go<S>1980</S> mourning<S>6937</S> all the day<S>3117</S> long. <sup>troubled: Heb. wried</sup>
KJV.7: For my loins<S>3689</S> are filled<S>4390</S> with a loathsome<S>7033</S> disease: and there is no soundness<S>4974</S> in my flesh<S>1320</S>.
KJV.8: I am feeble<S>6313</S> and sore<S>3966</S> broken<S>1794</S>: I have roared<S>7580</S> by reason of the disquietness<S>5100</S> of my heart<S>3820</S>.
KJV.9: Lord<S>136</S>, all my desire<S>8378</S> is before thee; and my groaning<S>585</S> is not hid<S>5641</S> from thee.
KJV.10: My heart<S>3820</S> panteth<S>5503</S>, my strength<S>3581</S> faileth<S>5800</S> me: as for the light<S>216</S> of mine eyes<S>5869</S>, it<S>1992</S> also is gone<S>369</S> from me. <sup>is gone: Heb. is not with me</sup>
KJV.11: My lovers<S>157</S> and my friends<S>7453</S> stand<S>5975</S> aloof from<S>5048</S> my sore<S>5061</S>; and my kinsmen<S>7138</S> stand<S>5975</S> afar off<S>7350</S>. <sup>sore: Heb. stroke</sup> <sup>my kinsmen: or, my neighbours</sup>
KJV.12: They also that seek<S>1245</S> after my life<S>5315</S> lay snares<S>5367</S> for me: and they that seek<S>1875</S> my hurt<S>7451</S> speak<S>1696</S> mischievous things<S>1942</S>, and imagine<S>1897</S> deceits<S>4820</S> all the day<S>3117</S> long.
KJV.13: But I, as a deaf<S>2795</S> man, heard<S>8085</S> not; and I was as a dumb man<S>483</S> that openeth<S>6605</S> not his mouth<S>6310</S>.
KJV.14: Thus I was as a man<S>376</S> that heareth<S>8085</S> not, and in whose mouth<S>6310</S> are no reproofs<S>8433</S>.
KJV.15: For in thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, do I hope<S>3176</S>: thou wilt hear<S>6030</S>, O Lord<S>136</S> my God<S>430</S>. <sup>in: or, thee do I wait for</sup> <sup>hear: or, answer</sup>
KJV.16: For I said<S>559</S>, Hear me, lest otherwise they should rejoice<S>8055</S> over me: when my foot<S>7272</S> slippeth<S>4131</S>, they magnify<S>1431</S> themselves against me.
KJV.17: For I am ready<S>3559</S> to halt<S>6761</S>, and my sorrow<S>4341</S> is continually<S>8548</S> before me. <sup>to halt: Heb. for halting</sup>
KJV.18: For I will declare<S>5046</S> mine iniquity<S>5771</S>; I will be sorry<S>1672</S> for my sin<S>2403</S>.
KJV.19: But mine enemies<S>341</S> are lively<S>2416</S>, and they are strong<S>6105</S>: and they that hate<S>8130</S> me wrongfully<S>8267</S> are multiplied<S>7231</S>. <sup>are lively: Heb. being living, are strong</sup>
KJV.20: They also that render<S>7999</S> evil<S>7451</S> for good<S>2896</S> are mine adversaries<S>7853</S>; because I follow<S>7291</S> the thing that good<S>2896</S> is.
KJV.21: Forsake<S>5800</S> me not, O LORD<S>3068</S>: O my God<S>430</S>, be not far<S>7368</S> from me.
KJV.22: Make haste<S>2363</S> to help<S>5833</S> me, O Lord<S>136</S> my salvation<S>8668</S>. <sup>to: Heb. for my help</sup>

ESV.1: O LORD, rebuke me not in your anger, nor discipline me in your wrath!
ESV.2: For your arrows have sunk into me, and your hand has come down on me.
ESV.3: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation; there is no health in my bones because of my sin.
ESV.4: For my iniquities have gone over my head; like a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.
ESV.5: My wounds stink and fester because of my foolishness,
ESV.6: I am utterly bowed down and prostrate; all the day I go about mourning.
ESV.7: For my sides are filled with burning, and there is no soundness in my flesh.
ESV.8: I am feeble and crushed; I groan because of the tumult of my heart.
ESV.9: O Lord, all my longing is before you; my sighing is not hidden from you.
ESV.10: My heart throbs; my strength fails me, and the light of my eyes — it also has gone from me.
ESV.11: My friends and companions stand aloof from my plague, and my nearest kin stand far off.
ESV.12: Those who seek my life lay their snares; those who seek my hurt speak of ruin and meditate treachery all day long.
ESV.13: But I am like a deaf man; I do not hear, like a mute man who does not open his mouth.
ESV.14: I have become like a man who does not hear, and in whose mouth are no rebukes.
ESV.15: But for you, O LORD, do I wait; it is you, O Lord my God, who will answer.
ESV.16: For I said, “Only let them not rejoice over me, who boast against me when my foot slips!”
ESV.17: For I am ready to fall, and my pain is ever before me.
ESV.18: I confess my iniquity; I am sorry for my sin.
ESV.19: But my foes are vigorous, they are mighty, and many are those who hate me wrongfully.
ESV.20: Those who render me evil for good accuse me because I follow after good.
ESV.21: Do not forsake me, O LORD! O my God, be not far from me!
ESV.22: Make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation!

NASB.1: A Psalm of David, for a memorial. O LORD, rebuke me not in Your wrath, And chasten me not in Your burning anger.
NASB.2: For Your arrows have sunk deep into me, And Your hand has pressed down on me.
NASB.3: There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your indignation; There is no health in my bones because of my sin.
NASB.4: For my iniquities are gone over my head; As a heavy burden they weigh too much for me.
NASB.5: My wounds grow foul [and] fester Because of my folly.
NASB.6: I am bent over and greatly bowed down; I go mourning all day long.
NASB.7: For my loins are filled with burning, And there is no soundness in my flesh.
NASB.8: I am benumbed and badly crushed; I groan because of the agitation of my heart.
NASB.9: Lord, all my desire is before You; And my sighing is not hidden from You.
NASB.10: My heart throbs, my strength fails me; And the light of my eyes, even that has gone from me.
NASB.11: My loved ones and my friends stand aloof from my plague; And my kinsmen stand afar off.
NASB.12: Those who seek my life lay snares [for me]; And those who seek to injure me have threatened destruction, And they devise treachery all day long.
NASB.13: But I, like a deaf man, do not hear; And [I am] like a mute man who does not open his mouth.
NASB.14: Yes, I am like a man who does not hear, And in whose mouth are no arguments.
NASB.15: For I hope in You, O LORD; You will answer, O Lord my God.
NASB.16: For I said, 'May they not rejoice over me, [Who], when my foot slips, would magnify themselves against me.'
NASB.17: For I am ready to fall, And my sorrow is continually before me.
NASB.18: For I confess my iniquity; I am full of anxiety because of my sin.
NASB.19: But my enemies are vigorous [and] strong, And many are those who hate me wrongfully.
NASB.20: And those who repay evil for good, They oppose me, because I follow what is good.
NASB.21: Do not forsake me, O LORD; O my God, do not be far from me!
NASB.22: Make haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation!

NLT.1:
O LORD, don’t rebuke me in your anger
or discipline me in your rage!
NLT.2:
Your arrows have struck deep,
and your blows are crushing me.
NLT.3:
Because of your anger, my whole body is sick;
my health is broken because of my sins.
NLT.4:
My guilt overwhelms me —
it is a burden too heavy to bear.
NLT.5:
My wounds fester and stink
because of my foolish sins.
NLT.6:
I am bent over and racked with pain.
All day long I walk around filled with grief.
NLT.7:
A raging fever burns within me,
and my health is broken.
NLT.8:
I am exhausted and completely crushed.
My groans come from an anguished heart.
NLT.9:
You know what I long for, Lord;
you hear my every sigh.
NLT.10:
My heart beats wildly, my strength fails,
and I am going blind.
NLT.11:
My loved ones and friends stay away, fearing my disease.
Even my own family stands at a distance.
NLT.12:
Meanwhile, my enemies lay traps to kill me.
Those who wish me harm make plans to ruin me.
All day long they plan their treachery.
NLT.13:
But I am deaf to all their threats.
I am silent before them as one who cannot speak.
NLT.14:
I choose to hear nothing,
and I make no reply.
NLT.15:
For I am waiting for you, O LORD.
You must answer for me, O Lord my God.
NLT.16:
I prayed, “Don’t let my enemies gloat over me
or rejoice at my downfall.”
NLT.17:
I am on the verge of collapse,
facing constant pain.
NLT.18:
But I confess my sins;
I am deeply sorry for what I have done.
NLT.19:
I have many aggressive enemies;
they hate me without reason.
NLT.20:
They repay me evil for good
and oppose me for pursuing good.
NLT.21:
Do not abandon me, O LORD.
Do not stand at a distance, my God.
NLT.22:
Come quickly to help me,
O Lord my savior.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David, for a memorial. Yahweh, don’t rebuke me in your wrath, neither chasten me in your hot displeasure.
WEB.2: For your arrows have pierced me, your hand presses hard on me.
WEB.3: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation, neither is there any health in my bones because of my sin.
WEB.4: For my iniquities have gone over my head. As a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.
WEB.5: My wounds are loathsome and corrupt, because of my foolishness.
WEB.6: I am pained and bowed down greatly. I go mourning all day long.
WEB.7: For my waist is filled with burning. There is no soundness in my flesh.
WEB.8: I am faint and severely bruised. I have groaned by reason of the anguish of my heart.
WEB.9: Lord, all my desire is before you. My groaning is not hidden from you.
WEB.10: My heart throbs. My strength fails me. As for the light of my eyes, it has also left me.
WEB.11: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my plague. My kinsmen stand far away.
WEB.12: They also who seek after my life lay snares. Those who seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and meditate deceits all day long.
WEB.13: But I, as a deaf man, don’t hear. I am as a mute man who doesn’t open his mouth.
WEB.14: Yes, I am as a man who doesn’t hear, in whose mouth are no reproofs.
WEB.15: For in you, Yahweh, do I hope. You will answer, Lord my God.
WEB.16: For I said, “Don’t let them gloat over me, or exalt themselves over me when my foot slips.”
WEB.17: For I am ready to fall. My pain is continually before me.
WEB.18: For I will declare my iniquity. I will be sorry for my sin.
WEB.19: But my enemies are vigorous and many. Those who hate me without reason are numerous.
WEB.20: They who also render evil for good are adversaries to me, because I follow what is good.
WEB.21: Don’t forsake me, Yahweh. My God, don’t be far from me.
WEB.22: Hurry to help me, Lord, my salvation.

CSB17.1:
A psalm of David for remembrance. ⓙ [1] LORD, do not punish me in your anger
or discipline me in your wrath. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
For your arrows have sunk into me,
and your hand has pressed down on me. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
There is no soundness in my body
because of your indignation;
there is no health [1] in my bones
because of my sin. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
For my iniquities have flooded over my head;
they are a burden too heavy for me to bear. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
My wounds are foul and festering
because of my foolishness. ⓐ
CSB17.6:
I am bent over and brought very low;
all day long I go around in mourning. ⓑ
CSB17.7:
For my insides are full of burning pain,
and there is no soundness in my body. ⓒ
CSB17.8:
I am faint and severely crushed;
I groan because of the anguish of my heart. ⓓ
CSB17.9:
Lord, my every desire is in front of you;
my sighing is not hidden from you. ⓔ
CSB17.10:
My heart races, my strength leaves me,
and even the light of my eyes has faded. [2] ⓕ
CSB17.11:
My loved ones and friends stand back from my affliction,
and my relatives stand at a distance. ⓖ
CSB17.12:
Those who intend to kill me set traps,
and those who want to harm me threaten to destroy me;
they plot treachery all day long. ⓗ
CSB17.13:
I am like a deaf person; I do not hear.
I am like a speechless person
who does not open his mouth. ⓘ
CSB17.14:
I am like a man who does not hear
and has no arguments in his mouth. ⓙ
CSB17.15:
For I put my hope in you, LORD;
you will answer me, my Lord, my God. ⓚ
CSB17.16:
For I said, “Don’t let them rejoice over me —
those who are arrogant toward me when I stumble.” ⓛ
CSB17.17:
For I am about to fall,
and my pain is constantly with me. ⓜ
CSB17.18:
So I confess my iniquity;
I am anxious because of my sin. ⓝ
CSB17.19:
But my enemies are vigorous and powerful; [3]
many hate me for no reason. ⓐ
CSB17.20:
Those who repay evil for good
attack me for pursuing good. ⓑ
CSB17.21:
LORD, do not abandon me;
my God, do not be far from me. ⓒ
CSB17.22:
Hurry to help me,
my Lord, my salvation. ⓓ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David; to bring to remembrance. O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your wrath,
Nor discipline me in Your burning anger.
AMP.2:
For Your arrows have sunk into me and penetrate deeply,
And Your hand has pressed down on me and greatly disciplined me.
AMP.3:
There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your indignation;
There is no health in my bones because of my sin.
AMP.4:
For my iniquities have gone over my head [like the waves of a flood];
As a heavy burden they weigh too much for me.
AMP.5:
My wounds are loathsome and foul
Because of my foolishness.
AMP.6:
I am bent over and greatly bowed down;
I go about mourning all day long.
AMP.7:
For my sides are filled with burning,
And there is no health in my flesh.
AMP.8:
I am numb and greatly bruised [deadly cold and completely worn out];
I groan because of the disquiet and moaning of my heart.
AMP.9:
Lord, all my desire is before You;
And my sighing is not hidden from You.
AMP.10:
My heart throbs violently, my strength fails me;
And as for the light of my eyes, even that has also gone from me.
AMP.11:
My loved ones and my friends stand aloof from my plague;
And my neighbors stand far away.
AMP.12:
Those who seek my life lay snares for me,
And those who seek to injure me threaten mischievous things and destruction;
They devise treachery all the day long.
AMP.13:
But I, like a deaf man, do not hear;
I am like a mute man who does not open his mouth.
AMP.14:
Yes, I am like a man who does not hear,
In whose mouth are no arguments.
AMP.15:
For in You, O LORD, I hope;
You will answer, O Lord my God.
AMP.16:
For I pray, "May they not rejoice over me,
Who, when my foot slips, would boast against me."
AMP.17:
For I am ready to fall;
My sorrow is continually before me.
AMP.18:
For I do confess my guilt and iniquity;
I am filled with anxiety because of my sin.
AMP.19:
But my [numerous] enemies are vigorous and strong,
And those who hate me without cause are many.
AMP.20:
They repay evil for good, they attack and try to kill me,
Because I follow what is good.
AMP.21:
Do not abandon me, O LORD;
O my God, do not be far from me.
AMP.22:
Make haste to help me,
O Lord, my Salvation.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Take a deep breath, God; calm down—
don't be so hasty with your punishing rod.
MSG.2:
Your sharp-pointed arrows of rebuke draw blood;
my backside smarts from your caning.
MSG.3:
I've lost twenty pounds in two months
because of your accusation.
My bones are brittle as dry sticks
because of my sin.
MSG.4:
I'm swamped by my bad behavior,
collapsed under gunnysacks of guilt.
MSG.5:
The cuts in my flesh stink and grow maggots
because I've lived so badly.
MSG.6:
And now I'm flat on my face
feeling sorry for myself morning to night.
MSG.7:
All my insides are on fire,
my body is a wreck.
MSG.8:
I'm on my last legs; I've had it—
my life is a vomit of groans.
MSG.9:
Lord, my longings are sitting in plain sight,
my groans an old story to you.
MSG.10:
My heart's about to break;
I'm a burned-out case.
Cataracts blind me to God and good;
MSG.11:
old friends avoid me like the plague.
My cousins never visit,
my neighbors stab me in the back.
MSG.12:
My competitors blacken my name,
devoutly they pray for my ruin.
MSG.13:
But I'm deaf and mute to it all,
ears shut, mouth shut.
MSG.14:
I don't hear a word they say,
don't speak a word in response.
MSG.15:
What I do, God, is wait for you,
wait for my Lord, my God—you will answer!
MSG.16:
I wait and pray so they won't laugh me off,
won't smugly strut off when I stumble.
MSG.17:
I'm on the edge of losing it—
the pain in my gut keeps burning.
MSG.18:
I'm ready to tell my story of failure,
I'm no longer smug in my sin.
MSG.19:
My enemies are alive and in action,
a lynch mob after my neck.
MSG.20:
I give out good and get back evil
from God-haters who can't stand a God-lover.
MSG.21:
Don't dump me, God;
my God, don't stand me up.
MSG.22:
Hurry and help me;
I want some wide-open space in my life!

NIV.1:
Psalm 38
A psalm of David. A petition.
O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger
or discipline me in your wrath.
NIV.2:
For your arrows have pierced me,
and your hand has come down upon me.
NIV.3:
Because of your wrath there is no health in my body;
my bones have no soundness because of my sin.
NIV.4:
My guilt has overwhelmed me
like a burden too heavy to bear.
NIV.5:
My wounds fester and are loathsome
because of my sinful folly.
NIV.6:
I am bowed down and brought very low;
all day long I go about mourning.
NIV.7:
My back is filled with searing pain;
there is no health in my body.
NIV.8:
I am feeble and utterly crushed;
I groan in anguish of heart.
NIV.9:
All my longings lie open before you, O Lord;
my sighing is not hidden from you.
NIV.10:
My heart pounds, my strength fails me;
even the light has gone from my eyes.
NIV.11:
My friends and companions avoid me because of my wounds;
my neighbors stay far away.
NIV.12:
Those who seek my life set their traps,
those who would harm me talk of my ruin;
all day long they plot deception.
NIV.13:
I am like a deaf man, who cannot hear,
like a mute, who cannot open his mouth;
NIV.14:
I have become like a man who does not hear,
whose mouth can offer no reply.
NIV.15:
I wait for you, O Lord;
you will answer, O Lord my God.
NIV.16:
For I said, “Do not let them gloat
or exalt themselves over me when my foot slips.”
NIV.17:
For I am about to fall,
and my pain is ever with me.
NIV.18:
I confess my iniquity;
I am troubled by my sin.
NIV.19:
Many are those who are my vigorous enemies;
those who hate me without reason are numerous.
NIV.20:
Those who repay my good with evil
slander me when I pursue what is good.
NIV.21:
O Lord, do not forsake me;
be not far from me, O my God.
NIV.22:
Come quickly to help me,
O Lord my Savior.

NRSVCE.1:
O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger,
or discipline me in your wrath.
NRSVCE.2:
For your arrows have sunk into me,
and your hand has come down on me.
NRSVCE.3:
There is no soundness in my flesh
because of your indignation;
there is no health in my bones
because of my sin.
NRSVCE.4:
For my iniquities have gone over my head;
they weigh like a burden too heavy for me.
NRSVCE.5:
My wounds grow foul and fester
because of my foolishness;
NRSVCE.6:
I am utterly bowed down and prostrate;
all day long I go around mourning.
NRSVCE.7:
For my loins are filled with burning,
and there is no soundness in my flesh.
NRSVCE.8:
I am utterly spent and crushed;
I groan because of the tumult of my heart.
NRSVCE.9:
O Lord, all my longing is known to you;
my sighing is not hidden from you.
NRSVCE.10:
My heart throbs, my strength fails me;
as for the light of my eyes — it also has gone from me.
NRSVCE.11:
My friends and companions stand aloof from my affliction,
and my neighbors stand far off.
NRSVCE.12:
Those who seek my life lay their snares;
those who seek to hurt me speak of ruin,
and meditate treachery all day long.
NRSVCE.13:
But I am like the deaf, I do not hear;
like the mute, who cannot speak.
NRSVCE.14:
Truly, I am like one who does not hear,
and in whose mouth is no retort.
NRSVCE.15:
But it is for you, O LORD, that I wait;
it is you, O Lord my God, who will answer.
NRSVCE.16:
For I pray, “Only do not let them rejoice over me,
those who boast against me when my foot slips.”
NRSVCE.17:
For I am ready to fall,
and my pain is ever with me.
NRSVCE.18:
I confess my iniquity;
I am sorry for my sin.
NRSVCE.19:
Those who are my foes without cause are mighty,
and many are those who hate me wrongfully.
NRSVCE.20:
Those who render me evil for good
are my adversaries because I follow after good.
NRSVCE.21:
Do not forsake me, O LORD;
O my God, do not be far from me;
NRSVCE.22:
make haste to help me,
O Lord, my salvation.

NET.1: O LORD, do not continue to rebuke me in your anger! Do not continue to punish me in your raging fury!
NET.2: For your arrows pierce me, and your hand presses me down.
NET.3: My whole body is sick because of your judgment; I am deprived of health because of my sin.
NET.4: For my sins overwhelm me; like a heavy load, they are too much for me to bear.
NET.5: My wounds are infected and starting to smell, because of my foolish sins.
NET.6: I am dazed and completely humiliated; all day long I walk around mourning.
NET.7: For I am overcome with shame and my whole body is sick.
NET.8: I am numb with pain and severely battered; I groan loudly because of the anxiety I feel.
NET.9: O Lord, you understand my heart’s desire; my groaning is not hidden from you.
NET.10: My heart beats quickly; my strength leaves me; I can hardly see.
NET.11: Because of my condition, even my friends and acquaintances keep their distance; my neighbors stand far away.
NET.12: Those who seek my life try to entrap me; those who want to harm me speak destructive words; all day long they say deceitful things.
NET.13: But I am like a deaf man — I hear nothing; I am like a mute who cannot speak.
NET.14: I am like a man who cannot hear and is incapable of arguing his defense.
NET.15: Yet I wait for you, O LORD! You will respond, O Lord, my God!
NET.16: I have prayed for deliverance, because otherwise they will gloat over me; when my foot slips they will arrogantly taunt me.
NET.17: For I am about to stumble, and I am in constant pain.
NET.18: Yes, I confess my wrongdoing, and I am concerned about my sins.
NET.19: But those who are my enemies for no reason are numerous; those who hate me without cause outnumber me.
NET.20: They repay me evil for the good I have done; though I have tried to do good to them, they hurl accusations at me.
NET.21: Do not abandon me, O LORD! My God, do not remain far away from me!
NET.22: Hurry and help me, O Lord, my deliverer!
